content: Before any worker brief, plan section, or implementation code names an Excalidraw API (prop, hook, factory, type, AppState field, lifecycle event), grep the vendored source under code/packages/excalidraw/ and code/packages/element/src/ FIRST. The plan literal may diverge from v0.18 reality; TypeScript will not catch it because ExcalidrawProps is loose enough to silently accept unknown props. Three failure instances captured: viewBackgroundColor (Wave 3a), customTools (Wave 3b), newElementWith (Wave 3b). Codified as project rule at .claude/rules/excalidraw-api.md. Mandatory greps: <Excalidraw> props → ExcalidrawProps in types.ts; AppState fields → interface AppState in types.ts; element factories → newElement.ts; imperative API methods → ExcalidrawImperativeAPI in types.ts. If grep returns nothing, plan is wrong; pick alternative before dispatching.

description: Excalidraw v0.18: viewBackgroundColor is NOT a top-level <Excalidraw> prop — it lives in AppState (default COLOR_PALETTE.white = #ffffff). TypeScript silently accepts <Excalidraw viewBackgroundColor='transparent'> because ExcalidrawProps is loose enough to allow unknown props. The prop is ignored and Excalidraw paints opaque white over the map layer. Caught by user screenshot mid-Wave-3a.
resolution: Pass via initialData.appState: <Excalidraw initialData={{ appState: { viewBackgroundColor: 'transparent' } }} />. Module-scope the literal so Excalidraw's mount-time read sees stable identity. Verified in code/apps/atlas-app/src/components/MapEditor.tsx EXCALIDRAW_INITIAL_DATA constant.

description: Excalidraw v0.18 has NO customTools prop on <Excalidraw>. Plan literally said 'use Excalidraw's custom tool registration' — there is no such API. The plan reference was hallucinated or carried from a different Excalidraw version. ExcalidrawProps surface (code/packages/excalidraw/types.ts:570-710) has renderTopRightUI / renderTopLeftUI / renderEmbeddable / onPointerDown but no customTools / customElementsConfig hook. Atlas-side tools cannot be registered with Excalidraw's tool system.
resolution: Atlasdraw tools dispatch independently of Excalidraw's tool system: AtlasdrawTool interface (code/packages/tools/src/types.ts) is OUR contract; useAtlasdrawTool hook owns activeAtlasTool state + ToolContext factory; an absolute-positioned interaction overlay above the Excalidraw layer captures pointerdown when an atlas-tool is active and dispatches to tool.onPointerDown. seedToElement bridge converts AtlasdrawElementSeed → ExcalidrawElement with full GeoCustomData. PinTool (code/packages/tools/src/PinTool.ts) is the first instance of this pattern.

description: Plan said 'use newElementWith or newTextElement' for creating fresh Excalidraw elements. newElementWith MUTATES an existing element (passes patch fields onto an element already in the scene) — it does NOT create from scratch. Using it for creation produces no element; subsequent updateScene calls don't see it.
resolution: Use the factory functions from @excalidraw/element (vendored at code/packages/element/src/newElement.ts): newElement(opts) for generic, newTextElement(opts & {text}) for text, newRectangleElement(opts) for rectangle. Inject via excalidrawAPI.updateScene({ elements: [...current, newEl], captureUpdate: 'immediately' }). Reference: code/apps/atlas-app/src/tools/seedToElement.ts uses newElement to build pin ellipses.

name: appstate-newElement-signals-actively-drafting
description: Excalidraw's AppState exposes a 'newElement' field (NonDeleted<ExcalidrawNonSelectionElement> | null, defined at code/packages/excalidraw/types.ts:291) that is non-null while the user is actively drafting a new element via mouse drag (between pointerDown and pointerUp). It clears to null when the draft is committed. This is the right signal to discriminate 'still drawing' from 'just finished' for any post-creation hook (e.g., useGeoAnchor stamps customData.geo only after appState.newElement clears, so the geo-anchor reflects the final bbox, not the first-frame click point).

name: atlasdraw-tools-dispatch-via-overlay
description: Atlasdraw's geo-aware tools (PinTool, future PolygonTool/LineTool) cannot register with Excalidraw v0.18 because customTools prop doesn't exist. Established pattern: (1) AtlasdrawTool interface in @atlasdraw/tools is OUR contract — pure data, no React/DOM/maplibre imports; tools implement onPointerDown(e, ctx) where ctx is a ToolContext façade. (2) An overlay div is rendered in atlas-app's MapEditor ABOVE the Excalidraw layer, mounted only when activeAtlasTool != null; it captures pointerdown and dispatches to tool.onPointerDown via useAtlasdrawTool's dispatcher. (3) After dispatch, the tool resets to null (one-shot). (4) seedToElement bridges AtlasdrawElementSeed → ExcalidrawElement, stamping full GeoCustomData (projection:'mercator', schemaVersion:1). PinTool is the reference implementation.

Excalidraw layer with pointer-events:auto (drawing-mode default in any tool except hand) captures WHEEL events along with pointer events, hiding them from the underlying MapLibre canvas's scrollZoom listener. User-visible symptom: scroll-to-zoom appears broken in any non-hand tool, and geo-anchored annotations appear to drift off their lat/lng anchor (because the map literally never zoomed; annotations are correctly glued to a stationary camera). Same root cause class as viewBackgroundColor (mx-52992e) and customTools: assuming Excalidraw composes as a transparent overlay without explicit wheel arbitration. atlasdraw-5afc.
resolution: Install a capture-phase wheel listener on the editor root that intercepts wheel and forwards delta directly to map.easeTo with canonical scrollZoom math: deltaY * (deltaMode===1 ? 25 : 1) * -0.0035, around=map.unproject(clientPos). DO NOT re-dispatch synthetic WheelEvent on map.getCanvas() — Safari and isTrusted checks make it cross-browser brittle (advisor flagged). Pass through ctrl/meta+wheel for browser pinch-zoom. Reference impl: code/apps/atlas-app/src/hooks/useMapWheelRouter.ts. Verified by 4 E2E cases in phase-1-geo-foundation.spec.ts (drawing-mode wheel zoom: 12->14.10, 0px drift). Touch pinch-zoom hits the same architectural issue via pointer events; defer to Phase 4 mobile/touch matrix.

title: wheel-event routing: capture-phase listener on root container → map.easeTo, not synthetic WheelEvent
rationale: Root cause of atlasdraw-5afc: Excalidraw layer pointer-events:auto in drawing mode captures wheel before MapLibre sees it. Fix: capture-phase wheel listener on root container dispatches map.easeTo directly (not synthetic WheelEvent — cross-browser safety). Modifier semantics: ctrl/meta+wheel passes through to Excalidraw zoom; shift+wheel intercepted for map. Firefox deltaMode=1 normalized via ×25 multiplier. Implemented in useMapWheelRouter.ts.

title: Phase 2 T02: adopt defaultScaleMode only — reject icon/PointerEvent/raw-ctx regressions
rationale: Plan-vs-impl audit (post-Wave4 opus audit) found 4 divergences: (1) icon: React.FC — plan's type is wrong, current impl has no icon field on AtlasdrawToolDef; (2) onPointerMove?/onPointerUp? — already present in canonical impl, plan would add duplicates; (3) raw maplibregl ctx — plan passed raw Map, impl correctly passes projected coords; (4) defaultScaleMode — genuinely missing from AtlasdrawToolDef, provides per-tool declarative scale behavior. Decision: surgical adoption of only defaultScaleMode addition, rejecting the other three as regressions. Rationale: per-tool declarative scale mode is a clean, additive contract extension; the other three would break existing interface invariants or introduce types that don't match vendored source. Rules out: wholesale plan adoption without scrub.
